summary: Justin Brennan introduces foundational financial terminology essential for analyzing multifamily deals: NOI (net operating income), cap rate (capitalization rate), IRR (internal rate of return), and COC (cash-on-cash return). Understanding how these metrics interact is critical for quickly evaluating whether a deal makes financial sense and how to interpret the numbers on financial statements.
takeaways: NOI = all income less all expenses, showing returns before debt service/mortgages
Cap rate = NOI divided by purchase price, answering 'what's my annualized return with zero debt?'
IRR = time value of money: cash-on-cash returns + appreciation/sale proceeds annualized over the hold period
COC = actual physical cash returned monthly/quarterly/yearly on the investment
IRR includes both current cash return AND future profit from sale/refinance, while COC is only current annual cash
Download the attached glossary file for complete definitions and backstory of all financial terms
These four metrics (NOI, cap rate, IRR, COC) are the 'main ones you gotta focus on' for deal analysis

skill_candidates.change: Learn to distinguish between COC (annual cash returns only) and IRR (cash returns + appreciation over time) to properly evaluate deal returns
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explicitly contrasts: 'if I'm getting 6% cash on cash, 8% cash on cash, 10% cash on cash, that's physical cash return' versus IRR which adds 'appreciation and profit that I'm going to get from a sale or a refinance' and 'take all of that money together...annualizing that'

skill_candidates.change: Master the cap rate formula (NOI ÷ purchase price) to quickly compare unlevered returns across markets and property types
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes cap rate's universal relevance: 'You'll hear it...What's the cap rate? What's the cap rate for the market? How is this area? What are cap rates for this type of property in the area? You'll kind of hear that all the time'

title: Lesson 1: Important Terms
